summary: Simon Späti shares his favorite productivity tools across several categories: instant translation (Google Translate's camera feature, Skype Translator), grammar correction (Grammarly), email management (Google Inbox with its bundling and snooze features), focus music (Focus@Will, Brain.fm, Noisli), noise-cancelling headphones (Bose QuietComfort), quick notes (Google Keep), and Chrome extensions (Momentum, OneTab). The post emphasizes how these tools collectively help manage communication, reduce distractions, and enable deep work. He particularly highlights the science behind focus music, citing studies showing how specially designed audio can enhance concentration without the distraction of regular music.
key_points: Google Translate's camera feature provides real-time translation of physical text, invaluable for living or traveling in foreign countries
Google Inbox's AI-powered bundling automatically organizes emails by trip, finances, and other categories, reducing email management overhead
Focus music apps like Brain.fm are designed by neuroscientists to enhance concentration without the emotional and intellectual engagement of regular music
Noise-cancelling headphones combined with focus music create an optimal environment for deep work
OneTab Chrome extension can save up to 95% of browser memory by converting open tabs into a stored list
money_quotes: It is crucial to get into a state of deep work if you want to get as much done and in the best quality.
Music is designed to connect with you intellectually and emotionally which lowers comprehension and creates a distraction.
Sometimes I get even a little headache as my brain is in such a deep state of concentration for an extended period.
If you travel or live in a foreign country, it can save your life!
Of course, Google will read your email content for that purpose, but they do that already so why not making use of it.
When your tabs are in the OneTab list, you will save up to 95% of memory.
key_insight: Achieving deep work requires intentionally engineering your environment with the right tools—from scientifically-designed focus music to noise-cancelling headphones to email management systems that minimize distraction.
